one_time_description: Share of income from First Nation Controlled Entities (Klahoose Investment Limited Partnership equity income), a large non-recurring gain driving the surplus
economic_notes: Klahoose First Nation has equity investments in Qathen Xwegus Management Corporation, Klahoose Investment Limited Partnership, Klahoose Forestry No.2 Limited Partnership, and BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP. Revenue sharing from gaming is a significant revenue source at $1,760,900. Forestry and range income of $85,000 noted. COVID-19 impact noted as uncertain.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021, not 2024. Province is BC. Three GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis: Qathen Xwegus Management Corporation (100%), Klahoose Investment Limited Partnership (99.99%), and Klahoose Forestry No.2 Limited Partnership (99.99%). The large annual surplus of $9.28M is driven almost entirely by $8.14M share of income from Klahoose Investment Limited Partnership. No schedule of remuneration was included in the extracted text. Transfer to deferred revenue of $78,791 recorded as other item reducing surplus. Related party transaction: $340,458 paid for housing construction to a family member of management. COVID-19 impact noted. total_government_transfers includes ISC $2,457,321 + CMHC $10,034 + DFO $112,748 + FNHA $552,961 + Province of BC $1,313,318 = $4,446,382 (minor rounding used $4,446,362 based on line items). osr calculated as total revenue minus government transfers.
